issue.title: Update uuid and tmp dependencies to fix security vulnerabilities
issue.body:
## Summary

ExcelJS has two transitive dependencies with known security vulnerabilities:
- `uuid@^8.3.0` — Missing buffer bounds check (CVE-2025-..., GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq)
- `tmp@^0.2.0` — Path traversal vulnerability (GHSA-9r2w-394v-53qc)

Both are locked in the latest version (4.4.0) and need updates.

## Current Situation

**exceljs@4.4.0 dependencies:**
```json
{
  "tmp": "^0.2.0",
  "uuid": "^8.3.0",
  "dayjs": "^1.8.34",
  "jszip": "^3.10.1",
  "saxes": "^5.0.1",
  "archiver": "^5.0.0",
  "fast-csv": "^4.3.1",
  "unzipper": "^0.10.11",
  "readable-stream": "^3.6.0"
}
```

**Impact:** Applications using ExcelJS cannot upgrade to `uuid@>=11.1.1` or `tmp@>=0.2.1` without downgrading exceljs or using `npm audit fix --force` (which can cause breaking changes).

## Request

Please upgrade to:
- `uuid@^11.1.1` (or appropriate major version compatible with your API)
- `tmp@^0.2.1+` (patched version)

This would allow downstream consumers to have secure dependency trees.

## Context

ExcelJS is used by thousands of applications. Many security scanners (Dependabot, npm audit, Snyk) flag these vulnerabilities. We cannot upgrade without forking or patching exceljs ourselves.

Thank you for considering this!

issue.title: Request to upgrade bundled tmp dependency to >= 0.2.6 to remediate GHSA-ph9p-34f9-6g65 (CVE-2026-44705)
issue.body:
Hi,

We are reaching out to request an upgrade of the tmp package that is bundled as a direct dependency of exceljs. The currently pinned version is affected by GHSA-ph9p-34f9-6g65 vulnerability.

Current state
exceljs@4.4.0 declares "tmp": "^0.2.0", which resolves to tmp@0.2.5. This is being flagged as vulnerability in projects that consume exceljs.

Requesting this upgrade to make the consuming applications vlnerability free.

Thanks,
Tibin Dominic

issue.title: Switch to ESM in (applicable) TypeScript packages
issue.body:
### Which application or package is this feature request for?

brokers, builders, collection, core, formatters, next, proxy, rest, structures, util, voice, ws

### Feature

Since all packages seem to now [require node `>=22.12.0`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Adiscordjs%2Fdiscord.js%20%22node%22%3A&type=code), I'm proposing dropping the CJS builds for at least the TypeScript packages (not proposing it for `discord.js` initially since it doesn't use a bundler and would be more work, likely affecting every file). Node `22.12.0` unflagged support for [`require`ing ESM](https://nodejs.org/api/modules.html#loading-ecmascript-modules-using-require), which is currently in a release candidate status with plans to make it stable quite soon. Currently all relevant TypeScript packages ship two versions of the same library, one in CJS and the other in ESM. This isn't ideal not just because it makes installations 2x larger, it also makes those packages vulnerable to the [dual package hazard](https://nodejs.org/en/learn/modules/publishing-a-package#the-dual-package-hazard). I see zero drawbacks to dropping CJS builds considering nothing would change for users and they would still be able to use the library the same way as always, even if they use CJS.

### Ideal solution or implementation

Considering `tsup` is used, this should be as easy as dropping `'cjs'` from the format array in the config file, and changing every applicable `package.json`.

### Alternative solutions or implementations

If for some strange reason it is decided to keep CJS builds despite the fact that users using the minimum required node version can `require(esm)` with absolutely no issues, at the very least the [`module-sync` export condition](https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#conditional-exports) could be used to avoid the dual package hazard.

> `"module-sync"` - matches no matter the package is loaded via `import`, `import()` or `require()`. The format is expected to be ES modules that does not contain top-level await in its module graph - if it does, `ERR_REQUIRE_ASYNC_MODULE` will be thrown when the module is `require()`-ed.

### Other context

I have a PR basically ready but I would like to first ask for clarification of which packages should be moved to ESM before submitting, especially around internal packages.

comment.body: Yeah, would be nice to go ESM-only

issue.title: feat: endpoint to embed downloads chart static svg

labels.name: needs review
labels.description: This PR is waiting for a review from a maintainer

issue.body: Resolves #2811 for the embedding aspect of the issue. This creates an endpoint that generates a static svg for download charts, from: - chart modal - compare page | page | closed | open | |------|-------|-------| | chart modal ||| | compare | || - dark or light theme can be toggled - URL can be copied from the input - small preview image using the same URL Important note regarding the data: - data corrections are ignored, because embedded static charts must show the raw data. - the local zoom state (using the chart's minimap) is ignored. Only the date filters are applied. - the embed only works for downloads for now. Refactoring: - common logic used both in the embed & the client chart is refactored into shared utils Rendering: - the rendered SVG will appear with the default font of the browser. Consumers can add styles to a wrapper, the chart will inherit the font-family. Other: - vue-data-ui is updated to latest, with support for static svg generation for the VueUiXy component ([release notes](https://github.com/graphieros/vue-data-ui/releases/tag/v3.21.0)). - VueUiXy is the only component with this feature for now. Should embeds be created for other chart components used on npmx, the library would need to be updated. I'm planning on gradually adding this feature to all mainstream chart types.

comment.body:
> > It also jumps in height, due to having `max-height: 90vh`, so when expanding it gets the height of `90vh`, and when not expanded, it has less height
> > I don't know what we can about it, but potentially we could set `height: 100%`, so it's always the height of `90vh`
> > Or we can just ignore it and leave it as is
> 
> @gameroman honestly, I was never happy with the chart modal in the first place. On some smaller screens, the modal opens already with cropped content and scrollbar, which is ugly. I think it should be in a separate page, maybe along future stats page, instead of a modal.

Yeah, a separate page would be nice, but that would be a separate PR

This is good to go then 🎉

There should be a trailing line but empty 😅 

```suggestion

```

issue.title: feat: endpoint to embed downloads chart static svg

labels.name: needs review
labels.description: This PR is waiting for a review from a maintainer

issue.body: Resolves #2811 for the embedding aspect of the issue. This creates an endpoint that generates a static svg for download charts, from: - chart modal - compare page | page | closed | open | |------|-------|-------| | chart modal ||| | compare | || - dark or light theme can be toggled - URL can be copied from the input - small preview image using the same URL Important note regarding the data: - data corrections are ignored, because embedded static charts must show the raw data. - the local zoom state (using the chart's minimap) is ignored. Only the date filters are applied. - the embed only works for downloads for now. Refactoring: - common logic used both in the embed & the client chart is refactored into shared utils Rendering: - the rendered SVG will appear with the default font of the browser. Consumers can add styles to a wrapper, the chart will inherit the font-family. Other: - vue-data-ui is updated to latest, with support for static svg generation for the VueUiXy component ([release notes](https://github.com/graphieros/vue-data-ui/releases/tag/v3.21.0)). - VueUiXy is the only component with this feature for now. Should embeds be created for other chart components used on npmx, the library would need to be updated. I'm planning on gradually adding this feature to all mainstream chart types.

comment.body:
It also jumps in height, due to having `max-height: 90vh`, so when expanding it gets the height of `90vh`, and when not expanded, it has less height

I don't know what we can about it, but potentially we could set `height: 100%`, so it's always the height of `90vh`

Or we can just ignore it and leave it as is

When expanding this, the width of container changes due to scrollbar appearing, which causes some jumps.

Could we add some style (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/scrollbar-gutter#stable) to the container to prevent it from doing that?

issue.title: discussion: esm-only
issue.body:
What is your opinion on going [ESM-only](https://areweesmyet.com)?

This would require a new major version, but would allow to update the `archiver` dependency, and significantly reduce install size.

If you are happy with that, I would be very glad to help!

comment.body: New Node.js version support `require(ESM)`, could that help here?

issue.title: ESM Support for Nx Plugins

labels.name: priority: medium
labels.description: Medium Priority (not high, not low priority)

issue.body:
<!-- Please do your best to fill out all of the sections below! -->
<!-- Use this issue type for concrete suggestions, otherwise, open a discussion type issue instead. -->

- [x] I'd be willing to implement this feature ([contributing guide](https://github.com/nrwl/nx/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md))


## Description
Nx plugins must be written in CJS

## Motivation
ESM is the future, and the way things are going this will be necessary at some point.

## Implementation path

Places that import nx plugins, must dynamically import them

- [ ] loadNxPlugins
- [ ] loadNxPlugin
- [ ] readGenerator
- [ ] readExecutor

This is difficult on our side, since readGenerator / readExecutor are on the `Workspaces` class, a public devkit API. Additionally, project inference capabilities make use of loadNxPlugins. This stuff gets hit in `getProjects(tree: Tree)`, `readProjectConfiguration(tree: Tree)` etc. Those are **_major_** public API points in terms of internal use and community plugin use, let alone workspace-generators or local plugins within an organization.

**_ALL_** of these APIs will need to go async only, representing a **_major_** breaking change.

comment.body: Would be nice to have this. Anything we could help with here?

issue.title: docs feature codeblock hyphenated lang regex
issue.body:
**Handle hyphenated fenced languages in `@example` blocks.**

These regexes only consume `\w`, so a valid fence like ```` ```glimmer-ts ```` is parsed as language `glimmer` and leaves `-ts` at the start of the code body. That breaks example rendering for some Shiki languages now loaded in `server/utils/shiki.ts`, instead of falling back cleanly.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_Originally posted by @coderabbitai[bot] in https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/pull/2382#discussion_r3042830927_

issue.title: ci: stale workflow not working

labels.name: pending triage
labels.description: New issue waiting for initial maintainer triage

issue.body: ### Describe the bug It has been over a month (30 days) since we integrated [the stale workflow](https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/blob/main/.github/workflows/stale.yml) in https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/pull/2580. But in all issues and PRs where a `"stale"` label got added, this label got instantly removed again: This happens because we have two jobs that run `action/stale` and interfere with each other because the run in parallel: - the PR job has the config `days-before-issue-stale: -1` -> removing all `"stale"` labels from issues (which have just been added by the issue job) - the issue job has the config `days-before-pr-stale: -1` -> removing all `"stale"` labels from PRs (which have just been added by the PR job) ### Additional context _No response_ ### Logs ```shell-script ```
